nodes.label: AI as learning tutor for skill acquisition
nodes.label_negative: AI as task doer that replaces learning
nodes.grounding_span: I use it to help me learn things that I can use on my own

nodes.label: AI as data analytics executor
nodes.grounding_span: I never have it do any data analytics for me - I think this is a horrible idea

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy for simple queries
nodes.label_negative: AI unreliability and looping errors for complex tasks
nodes.grounding_span: for simple queries and explanations like this, ChatGPT has a pretty high accuracy

nodes.label: Human experiential learning and intuition
nodes.label_negative: AI pattern matching without experiential understanding
nodes.grounding_span: it wouldn't be able to connect these quantified error cases with the actual thinking process a labeler would experience

nodes.label: AI as skill replacement leading to atrophy
nodes.label_negative: AI as skill augmentation preserving human ability
nodes.grounding_span: relying on AI for that stuff causes you to forget, or even worse...not develop those neural pathways at all

nodes.label: Corporate outsourcing to AI for profit
nodes.label_negative: Corporate investment in human workers and skills
nodes.grounding_span: corporations are just going to keep following profit motives and keep outsourcing to AI even if the results are bad

nodes.label: Personal competence and skill mastery
nodes.grounding_span: I can't stress how fast I had to learn and ramp up

nodes.label: Job security and meaningful work
nodes.grounding_span: people need work for both monetary and psychological reasons

nodes.label: Human intuition and experiential understanding
nodes.grounding_span: relies a lot on intuition and experience

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as learning tool
nodes.grounding_span: being able to effectively use AI as a learning tool as well as my brain allowed me to do that

nodes.label: Strong opposition to AI doing analytics
nodes.grounding_span: I never have it do any data analytics for me - I think this is a horrible idea

nodes.label: Cautious trust in AI for simple queries
nodes.grounding_span: I've had generally good results because I know how to tailor my queries to its strong points and avoid the weak points

nodes.label: Anxiety about job replacement by AI
nodes.grounding_span: this is something I flat out panic about very often

nodes.label: Pride in own learning ramp-up
nodes.grounding_span: reading that makes me a bit proud of myself lol

nodes.label: Deliberate query tailoring to AI strengths
nodes.grounding_span: I know how to tailor my queries to its strong points and avoid the weak points

nodes.label: Repetition-based learning with active thinking
nodes.grounding_span: through repetition...and thinking while I do it (as opposed to copying and pasting)
